summary: High‑energy Europe ’72 show captured on a clean soundboard recording with a few minor hiss and distortion quirks.
review: The May 23 1972 Lyceum performance is celebrated for its soaring Dark Star, emotive Morning Dew, and a string of classic Dead staples (Mr. Charlie, St. Stephen, Comes‑a‑Time, Uncle John’s Band). Reviewers rave about the warm, clear soundboard mix, though a few note slight hiss in the Dark Star jam and a scratchy Morning Dew segment. Overall the music and recording are deemed highly enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5‑star ratings, praising both performance and sound quality; only a handful cite minor audio flaws, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and clear with warm ambience; occasional digital‑tune hiss in the Dark Star section and a scratchy distortion on Morning Dew are mentioned. Minor track‑order metadata issue noted, but no major dropouts.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Rockin' Pneumonia & The Boogie Woogie Flu
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows naturally from tight early‑song work into extended second‑set jams, highlighted by a half‑hour Dark Star that builds to an emotional Morning Dew and closes with uplifting classics.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for “out of this world” guitar tones and fanning during the Dark Star jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for laying down a warm, menacing bass foundation that underpins the improvisations.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment within Dark Star is described as wild, expansive and reminiscent of a “Fantasia”‑style passage.
